# Evidence at a glance {#sec-evidence .unnumbered}

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8). Scaffold — the table will be generated from the model CSVs.
:::

{{< include _report_data.qmd >}}

*[Scaffold]* A single table with **one row per outcome**, intended as the spine a reader can take in without the chapter sequence (and a seed for the plain-language publication). Columns:

| Outcome | ITT effect $\tau$ (items, 95% CrI) | $P(\tau>0)$ | $P(\text{benefit} \ge \delta)$ | Within-person (DiD) | Evidence label |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Word reading | *[from CSV]* | | | | |
| Letter–sound knowledge | | | | | |
| … | | | | | |

The table is auto-generated by a shared code chunk reading `rope_summary.csv` / `tau_summary.csv` / `did_summary.csv` across the ITT suite through the shared data-access helpers in `_report_data.qmd`, so the table and the per-chapter numbers cannot drift and **no value is hand-entered**. Evidence labels qualify the *evidence*, never the effect size (@sec-bridge). Until the suite is fitted, each cell degrades to a visible *pending fit* marker — for example:

```{python}
#| echo: false
show_or_pending(load_summary("lrpitt10", "rope_summary"), "the word-reading ITT effect card")
```
